purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.body:
## Purpose

Use this protocol when the user asks to remove, uninstall, delete, or disable SocratexAI from a configured project.

The agent should remove the pipeline through the installed remover script instead of manually deleting files.

command.title: Command
command.body:
## Command

Prefer:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File SocratexAI/tools/pipeline/remove_pipeline.ps1 -TargetPath .
```

Use `-DryRun` first when the user asks what would be removed.

Use `-RestoreOldDirectives` when existing directive snapshots such as `AGENTS.md.old` should be restored.

Use `-RemoveLocalWorkingFiles` only when the user explicitly wants branch-scoped local working memory removed too.

workflow.title: Workflow
workflow.body:
## Workflow

1. Read root `SOCRATEX.md`.
2. Read installed pipeline config when available.
3. Explain that removal will use `SocratexAI/tools/pipeline/remove_pipeline.ps1`.
4. Run a dry run if the request is ambiguous or high-risk.
5. Run the remover script.
6. Report removed paths, preserved paths, restored directives, and any manual cleanup still needed.

default_preservation.title: Default Preservation
default_preservation.body:
## Default Preservation

By default, preserve:

- user source files,
- non-pipeline documentation,
- project history,
- `ignored/ai-socratex/` branch working files,
- unrelated `.aiassistant/` content.

By default, remove:

- root `SOCRATEX.md`,
- `SocratexAI/`,
- `.aiassistant/socratex/`,
- thin directive pointers generated by SocratexAI when safe to identify.

safety.title: Safety
safety.body:
## Safety

Never remove project source files.

Never recursively remove a path unless it is verified inside the target project and matches a SocratexAI-owned path.

If ownership is unclear, preserve the path and report it.
